title: Ave Maria
summary: The repository XML is a local copy of University of Michigan CME item CME00097, a keyed encoding of Wilhelm Heuser's complete 1904 Anglia article ‘Ave Maria.’ Heuser's comparative edited selection prints five anonymous medieval texts: a Middle English hymn from Bodleian MS Douce 126, an Anglo-Norman hymn from Cambridge University Library MS Gg.1.1, a four-line Middle English salutation and a longer Middle English hymn from CUL MS Gg.4.32, and a Latin hymn from the same CUL manuscript. The complete journal volume and complete CUDL image inventory for Gg.1.1 are cached; no complete public facsimile was verified for Douce 126 or Gg.4.32.
review_notes: The graph models five distinct medieval textual items inside one modern edited selection; it does not merge the article, its texts, their witnesses, digital encodings, or facsimiles.

Heuser's two Gg.4.32 section headings both cite fol. 14v after calling the manuscript unfoliated. M. R. James and DIMEV expose additional legacy/current locators, so each source's locator is preserved without a synthetic one-to-one crosswalk.

Access and rights statements apply only to the named entity or component. Public-domain medieval wording does not automatically determine rights or provider terms for modern photographs, metadata, editions, or encodings.
Negative whole-facsimile findings are date-bounded to 2026-07-13 and do not prove that no private, offline, or subsequently published reproduction exists.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for CME00097
entities.description: The XML artifact consumed by this repository's conversion and publication workflow, supplied through the Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.
notes: IDG/@ID, BIBNO, and VID all identify CME00097.
The file's TEXT/@LANG omits the encoded Anglo-Norman section; the manifest records xno from direct textual and manuscript evidence.

entities.label: University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse item CME00097
entities.description: A public keyed and structurally marked-up digital text of Heuser's complete 1904 article.
notes: The current U-M item identifies the source as Heuser's Anglia 27 article and exposes the complete web text.
The collection describes its later additions as manually keyed, proofread, and marked up with basic TEI semantics.

entities.label: Heuser 1904 edition

bibliographic.citation: Wilhelm Heuser, ‘Ave Maria,’ Anglia: Zeitschrift für englische Philologie 27 (Neue Folge 15) (1904): 320–330. https://doi.org/10.1515/angl.1904.1904.27.320.
bibliographic.title: Ave Maria

notes: Section 1 occupies printed pages 320–323; Section 2 pages 323–325; Section 3 pages 326–329; and Section 4 pages 329–330.
The edition includes German comparative commentary, manuscript readings, five complete medieval verse items, and a Cambridge dateline with Heuser's signature.

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS Douce 126
entities.description: The manuscript witness for Heuser's Section 1 Middle English Ave Maria. The target poem is on fols. 92r–92v; the sole Digital Bodleian image is a non-overlapping slide of fols. 68v–69r.

date_statements.label: manuscript production
date_statements.value: first half of the fifteenth century

notes: The Digital Bodleian record describes an English manuscript of the first half of the fifteenth century.
Heuser and DIMEV place the selected hymn on fols. 92r–92v.

entities.label: Digital Bodleian selected image for MS Douce 126
entities.description: A one-canvas digital object derived from a 35mm slide or filmstrip. It reproduces fols. 68v–69r, not the CME00097 target on fols. 92r–92v.
entities.reproduction_format: One IIIF canvas reproducing a double-page opening from a 35mm slide or filmstrip
notes: Coverage is partial and has zero verified overlap with the selected poem.

entities.label: Cambridge University Library MS Gg.1.1
entities.description: The manuscript witness for Heuser's Section 2 Anglo-Norman Ave Maria. Direct inspection of CUDL images confirms the poem begins at the foot of fol. 264r, fills fol. 264v, and ends in the first two lines of fol. 265r.

date_statements.label: manuscript production
date_statements.value: first half of the fourteenth century, after 1307

notes: CUDL describes a trilingual compendium made in England, predominantly Anglo-Norman with Middle English and Latin.
The target fols. 264r–265r are written as a single-column verse sequence.

entities.label: Cambridge Digital Library complete image inventory for MS Gg.1.1
entities.description: CUDL's 1,281-canvas color facsimile of the complete published manuscript image sequence. The locally cached bundle preserves the provider manifest, inventory, and every exposed canvas image at a practical 1600-pixel-wide derivative.
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation 2 manifest with 1,281 color JPEG canvas images
notes: The complete-provider-inventory claim concerns every canvas CUDL publishes; it does not assert preservation-master resolution or the presence of unexposed physical surfaces.
The work occupies canvases 535–537; canvas 537 is shared with the opening of the next manuscript text.

entities.label: Cambridge University Library MS Gg.4.32
entities.description: The manuscript witness for Heuser's Section 3 four-line English salutation, the fifteen-stanza English hymn, and the Section 4 Latin hymn. Its multiple historical/current locator systems have not been fully reconciled.

holding.notes: No exact public CUL item catalogue page was verified. The linked Biblissima record is a scholarly aggregation, while Cambridge's repository supplies M. R. James's description.

physical_description.format: Codex; double columns; 32–37 lines per page
notes: James describes many hands and calls the London connection strong while expressing doubt about an apparent Norwich ownership clue.
James locates the Latin Ave at legacy fol. 14v/current fol. 22v and lists the English Ave immediately after it; DIMEV gives f. 15v for the four-line salutation and ff. 23vb–24va for the long hymn.

entities.label: M. R. James's unpublished description of CUL MS Gg.4.32
entities.description: Cambridge's repository publishes a four-page transcription of M. R. James's unpublished manuscript description, including physical description, contents, and dual foliation references.

entities.label: Biblissima record for CUL MS Gg.4.32
entities.description: A scholarly aggregation of Jonas manuscript data. Its generated IIIF endpoint is an empty Collection with no members or canvases, so it is metadata rather than a facsimile.

entities.label: Digital Index of Middle English Verse v1.0
entities.description: The 2025 versioned DIMEV data release. Records 1728, 1731, and 1680 describe the three Middle English items represented in Heuser's selection and cite their witnesses and editions.
notes: Record 1728 corresponds to IMEV/NIMEV 1059; record 1731 to 1062; and record 1680 to 1024.
DIMEV's aabb classification for record 1728 conflicts with Heuser's statement that its quatrains use continuous rhyme and should be checked against fols. 92r–92v.

agents.name: Digital Index of Middle English Verse

scope.notes: The identifiers, title, divisions, text, notes, and page milestones match. The exact transfer date and any intermediate normalization step are not documented.

scope.notes: The selection does not reproduce the CME00097 work portion at fols. 92r–92v.

object.description: ‘Hail Maiden of Maidens through word conceiving’
The four-line English Ave Maria and fifteen-stanza English hymn

object.description: Heuser's Middle English texts on pages 320–323 and 326–329

scope.notes: DIMEV is a later catalog and supporting source, not an upstream source of the 1904 edition.

editorial_practices.description: Heuser organizes five complete medieval items from three manuscripts into four comparative sections, with the four-line English salutation serving as the heading to the longer English hymn.

editorial_practices.representation: Expanded reading in the text with manuscript form or explanation in a note
editorial_practices.description: Heuser explicitly notes individual expansions and manuscript readings, including the Section 1 expansion of a doubled-l sign as ‘lle’ and Section 2 notes for abbreviated manuscript forms.
editorial_practices.scope: Documented local examples only; not asserted as a complete global policy

editorial_practices.description: The 2016 revision description records title proofing, structural review, correction and movement of page and manuscript milestones, conversion of headings to headnotes or quoted lines, and other validation-oriented changes.
notes: The schema lacks a general structural-markup practice, so this record uses selection only as the nearest controlled term; the description is authoritative for the specific revisions.

access.access_method: Digital Bodleian viewer or IIIF manifest

local_copies.label: Digital Bodleian IIIF manifest for the one-image Douce 126 selection
notes: The manifest identifies exactly one canvas, fols. 68v–69r. No image bytes were cached because the published selection has no overlap with CME00097 and is not a whole-manuscript surrogate.
Coverage is partial and excludes the target fols. 92r–92v.

access.access_method: Audit the exact Digital Bodleian object and its IIIF canvas inventory
notes: The official object contains one unrelated canvas, not the target folios or the whole codex.
The Bodleian FAQ says Digital Bodleian cannot digitize items on request; mediated copying and Imaging Services remain separate routes.

access.access_method: Request mediated special-collections copies or ask Imaging Services about a complete-object reproduction, subject to condition and quotation
access.format: Format, coverage, resolution, and permitted reuse by quotation and written terms
notes: For a complete-codex request, specify covers or boards, spine, pastedowns, flyleaves, blanks, and every manuscript leaf; identify fols. 92r–92v separately as the work portion.
Requestability does not guarantee that complete digitization is feasible or that supplied images may be redistributed.

access.access_method: Cambridge Digital Library viewer or IIIF Presentation manifest

local_copies.label: Complete CUDL MS Gg.1.1 IIIF bundle at 1600px
notes: The bundle contains the provider manifest, an auditable inventory, and all 1,281 canvas JPEGs requested at width 1600 pixels without intentional upscaling.
Completeness is relative to the full CUDL canvas inventory, not to preservation-master resolution or unexposed physical surfaces.

work_portion.label: Anglo-Norman Ave Maria edited in CME00097 Section 2
notes: Canvas 537/fol. 265r contains the poem's final two lines and the opening of the next manuscript item.

access.access_method: Cambridge repository landing page or exact PDF download

access.access_method: Biblissima record and generated IIIF Collection

local_copies.label: Biblissima generated empty IIIF Collection for CUL MS Gg.4.32
notes: The JSON is an sc:Collection with members:[] and no canvases; it is not a manuscript facsimile.

access.format: HTML catalog record and metadata-only IIIF Collection

access.access_method: Audit exact Biblissima aggregation, its generated IIIF Collection, and the expected CUDL identifier
notes: The generated Biblissima IIIF endpoint has no members or canvases, and no functioning CUDL object for MS-GG-00004-00032 was verified.
This is a dated negative finding, not proof that the manuscript has never been photographed or microfilmed.

access.access_method: Submit the CUL online imaging and permissions request form for private study or other use; quotation follows collection-care review
access.format: Normally uncompressed 600 ppi RGB JPEG, or TIFF if requested; coverage and feasibility by quotation
notes: CUL currently states a minimum order fee of GBP 25 before VAT and separate licensing requirements for publication; prices and feasibility can change.
For a complete-codex request, specify covers or boards, spine, pastedowns, flyleaves, blanks, and every leaf, then identify the Ave Maria portions separately.
Completing the form is not an obligation to purchase.

access.access_method: Consult in the Special Collections Reading Room as a registered Library user; contact the Library in advance to confirm availability

rights.component: The CME00097 digital text and materials presented by University of Michigan
rights.basis: The U-M collection expressly states that its materials are in the public domain in the United States and applies the DPLA No Copyright–United States statement.
notes: No Copyright–United States is a jurisdiction-specific rights statement, not an affirmative worldwide license.

rights.component: Upstream CME transcription and underlying 1904 source content represented in the local XML
rights.basis: The local artifact is supported as a copy of U-M CME00097, which U-M marks No Copyright–United States; the immediate edition was published in 1904.
notes: This does not override repository licenses for project-authored code, metadata, formatting, or outputs.

rights.component: The 1904 edition, transcription, selection, and editorial commentary
rights.basis: The article was published in 1904, well before the current U.S. public-domain cutoff for published works.
notes: The author's verified death date was not established in this review, so life-plus-term status outside the United States remains unresolved.

rights.component: Any protectable selection, transcription choices, notes, or other editorial contribution
rights.basis: The article's publication date is known, but a reliable death date for this Wilhelm Heuser was not established in the reviewed authority evidence; applicable terms and originality thresholds vary by jurisdiction.

rights.component: The anonymous medieval wording represented by all five texts, including the items in CUL MSS Gg.1.1 and Gg.4.32
rights.basis: The textual contents are medieval and far outside any U.S. copyright term.
notes: This statement concerns the historical wording, not modern photographs, scans, editions, transcriptions, metadata, or provider terms.
The schema attaches this shared statement to one entity; its component text explicitly includes the corresponding material in both CUL witnesses.

rights.component: The specific Internet Archive digital surrogate of Anglia volume 27
rights.basis: Internet Archive item metadata marks the volume possible-copyright-status NOT_IN_COPYRIGHT and copyright-region US.
notes: The provider metadata is recorded as its assertion and does not replace independent jurisdiction-specific analysis.

rights.component: DIMEV v1.0 data files and documentation
rights.basis: The Zenodo record identifies the release license as Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International.

rights.component: The one published Digital Bodleian image of fols. 68v–69r and its manifest attribution
rights.basis: The item-specific IIIF manifest attributes the photograph to Bodleian Libraries and states CC BY-NC 4.0.
notes: This license covers the exact published selection, not an unverified complete Douce 126 facsimile or future supplied images.

rights.component: Any new photographs or scans supplied in response to a Bodleian reproduction request
rights.jurisdiction: Provider terms and intended place of reuse
rights.basis: No supplied images or item-specific delivery and reuse terms are present. The public partial image's CC BY-NC license cannot be generalized to a future order.

rights.component: The CUDL downloadable manuscript images and metadata represented by the exact item manifest
rights.basis: The item-specific IIIF manifest states that downloadable images and metadata are licensed CC BY-NC 3.0 and attributes zooming images to Cambridge University Library.
notes: The noncommercial restriction matters for a repository designed to support both noncommercial and commercial print-on-demand uses; the private cache must not be copied into public builds without compatible permission.

rights.component: The Cambridge repository item and four-page transcription of M. R. James's description
rights.basis: The repository item provides a Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International license link and item-level license statement.

rights.component: Any new photographs or scans supplied by Cambridge University Library
rights.jurisdiction: Provider terms and intended place of reuse
rights.basis: No item-specific supplied images or license are present. CUL's general terms distinguish private-study copies from publication use and require prior written permission for reproduction.
notes: Preserve any quotation, invoice, declaration, and license received before redistributing supplied images or incorporating them into a public or commercial workflow.

evidence.citation: CME00097 XML source in the pinned CME submodule.
evidence.locator: IDG, BIBNO, VID, revision description, and complete TEXT
evidence.summary: The 23,640-byte XML identifies CME00097 and contains Heuser's complete article with four sections, five medieval textual items, German commentary, printed-page breaks, manuscript-folio milestones, notes, dateline, and signature.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, ‘Ave Maria / [ed. W. Heuser],’ CME00097.
evidence.locator: Item information and complete web text
evidence.summary: The official item identifies Heuser, Halle, Max Niemeyer, 1904, links the stable CME00097 handle, exposes the complete text, and repeats the collection's public-domain statement.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse collection page.
evidence.locator: Collection description and Rights and Permissions
evidence.summary: The collection describes manual keying, proofreading, basic TEI markup, and bulk XML availability; it states that the materials are public domain in the United States and applies No Copyright–United States.

evidence.citation: Wilhelm Heuser, ‘Ave Maria,’ Anglia 27 (1904), 320–330; complete volume scan.
evidence.locator: Printed pages 320–330; PDF pages 330–340; IA leaves n329–n339
evidence.summary: Direct inspection confirms the complete article, its five texts, section-level manuscript statements, bibliography, page and folio locators, editorial notes, Cambridge dateline, and Heuser signature. The volume title page confirms Anglia 27/N.F. 15, Max Niemeyer, Halle, 1904.
The downloaded item metadata identifies the complete volume files and records possible-copyright-status NOT_IN_COPYRIGHT with copyright-region US.

evidence.citation: Gemeinsame Normdatei record 11678380X for Wilhelm Heuser, Anglist and Oberlehrer.
evidence.summary: The authority record distinguishes the Anglist Wilhelm Heuser and provides a stable identifier but did not establish a reliable death date in this review.

evidence.citation: The Digital Index of Middle English Verse: Data Files and Documentation, version 1.0 (2025), Zenodo 10.5281/zenodo.14585961.
evidence.locator: Records.xml records 1728, 1731, and 1680; Manuscripts.xml BodDou126, CULGg432; Bibliography.xml Heuser1904b
evidence.summary: The versioned CC BY 4.0 data identifies incipits, subjects, verse forms, witness loci, IMEV/NIMEV numbers, and edition citations for the three Middle English items. Record 1728 gives Douce 126 ff. 92r–92v; record 1731 gives Gg.4.32 f. 15v; record 1680 gives Gg.4.32 ff. 23vb–24va.
notes: The downloaded release ZIP contains commit 5ad5f592868471b44fc72f6e2f42db419780b42c.

evidence.citation: Digital Bodleian, Bodleian Library MS. Douce 126, object 5a20243c-877d-41e1-b379-5ccbb876f08f.
evidence.locator: IIIF manifest metadata and sole canvas labeled fol. 068v–069r
evidence.summary: The official record supplies the shelfmark, first-half fifteenth-century date, English origin, parchment support, exact catalog link, one-canvas partial image inventory, and item-specific CC BY-NC 4.0 attribution. The canvas does not include target fols. 92r–92v.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, Digital Bodleian FAQ and Special Collections copying services.
evidence.summary: The FAQ states that Digital Bodleian cannot digitize items on request and links mediated special-collections copying and professional Imaging Services as the appropriate request routes.

evidence.citation: Cambridge Digital Library, Trilingual compendium of texts (Cambridge, University Library, MS Gg.1.1), IIIF manifest.
evidence.locator: Manifest metadata, attribution, and complete 1,281-canvas sequence
evidence.summary: The official manifest supplies classmark, title, date after 1307 in the first half of the fourteenth century, England origin, parchment support, extent, multilingual contents, complete image sequence, Heuser bibliography, and CC BY-NC 3.0 terms for downloadable images and metadata.

evidence.citation: Direct inspection of cached CUDL MS Gg.1.1 canvases 535–538.
evidence.summary: Canvas 535/fol. 264r shows the poem opening ‘Ave tresduce Marie’ at the foot of the leaf; canvas 536/fol. 264v contains its middle; canvas 537/fol. 265r contains its final two lines before a new text; canvas 538/fol. 265v belongs to the following text. This verifies work coverage as canvases 535–537.

evidence.citation: M. R. James, Unpublished description of Cambridge, University Library, MS Gg.4.32, transcription published by Cambridge University Library, 2020, DOI 10.17863/CAM.67837.
evidence.locator: Complete four-page description, especially contents item 8
evidence.summary: The description supplies Gg.4.32's title, languages, vellum support, dimensions, 139-leaf extent, hands, fourteenth-century dating, contents, dual foliation, and the Latin and English Ave Maria sequence. The repository labels the item CC BY 4.0.

evidence.citation: Biblissima+, Cambridge, Cambridge University Library, MS Gg.4.32.
evidence.locator: Identity, holding repository, fourteenth-century Jonas date, contents, and source attribution
evidence.summary: The aggregation confirms the current classmark and repository and reports a fourteenth-century date from Jonas. It is scholarly aggregation evidence, not a holding-institution item record.

evidence.citation: Biblissima generated IIIF Collection for CUL MS Gg.4.32.
evidence.summary: The retrieved 785-byte Presentation 2 document is an sc:Collection with @id null and members:[], not a manuscript image manifest. It therefore supports metadata availability and the dated negative facsimile audit, not digitization.

evidence.citation: Cambridge University Library, Image Supply and Reproduction and Pricing.
evidence.summary: CUL provides an online imaging and permissions form, distinguishes private-study from publication uses, quotes after collection-care review, describes standard file delivery, and currently states a GBP 25 minimum order before VAT.

evidence.citation: Cambridge University Library, Terms and Conditions for Image Supply and Reproduction.
evidence.locator: Copyright, Permission to Reproduce, and Use of Copies
evidence.summary: CUL's general terms state that private-study copies require a declaration and that supplied material may not be reproduced without prior written permission; item-specific invoices and schedules form part of the agreement.

evidence.citation: Cambridge University Library, Use Special Collections.
evidence.summary: CUL states that medieval manuscripts are consulted in the Special Collections Reading Room, which is open to registered users, and recommends advance contact to confirm availability.

evidence.citation: U.S. Copyright Office, Circular 15A, Duration of Copyright.
evidence.summary: The Copyright Office's term guidance supports the U.S. public-domain assessment for a work first published in 1904.

open_questions.question: How do Heuser's unfoliated/working references, M. R. James's legacy and bracketed foliation, and DIMEV's loci map onto the current leaves of CUL MS Gg.4.32?
next_steps: Ask Cambridge University Library whether a current foliation concordance exists.
If images are supplied, align incipits, explicits, column boundaries, and every folio label against Heuser and DIMEV.
notes: A repository-confirmed crosswalk or complete facsimile is needed to give exact modern work portions for the two English items and the Latin hymn without conflating numbering systems.

open_questions.question: Can the Bodleian supply or identify a complete reproduction of MS Douce 126, including target fols. 92r–92v?
next_steps: Ask Special Collections and Imaging Services about existing complete photography or a complete-object order.
Request fols. 92r–92v separately only if a complete-object copy is infeasible, while preserving the narrower coverage status.
notes: The public Digital Bodleian selection has no target overlap, so manuscript spelling, abbreviation, layout, and DIMEV's rhyme classification cannot yet be checked against images.

open_questions.question: Does Cambridge University Library hold an existing complete digital or microfilm surrogate of MS Gg.4.32 that is not publicly indexed?
next_steps: Submit a CUL enquiry or imaging-form request for the complete codex and ask whether existing photography can reduce cost.
Obtain item-specific reuse terms before placing any supplied images in a public repository or publication workflow.
notes: The empty Biblissima IIIF Collection cannot resolve foliation, visual layout, completeness, or Heuser's manuscript-based editorial interventions.

open_questions.question: Why does DIMEV v1.0 classify record 1728 as aabb when Heuser describes continuous rhyme and the edited quatrains appear monorhymed?
next_steps: Inspect a manuscript facsimile or repository-supplied images of fols. 92r–92v.
Check Saupe's 1998 edition and any DIMEV revision history for an explanation or correction.
notes: The discovery metadata should not silently choose a rhyme pattern until Douce 126 fols. 92r–92v or a later critical discussion is checked.

open_questions.question: What is the reliable death date of the Anglist Wilhelm Heuser who edited the 1904 article?
next_steps: Consult a biographical authority or archival personnel record that clearly identifies the Anglist by profession and works.
Recalculate relevant non-U.S. terms only after identity and death date are securely established.
notes: The 1904 article is public domain in the United States, but life-plus-term calculations outside the United States remain incomplete.
